evidence: "Damn this was a tense movie. More a suspense or thriller than a horror film... One of the best psychological thrillers I've seen in a long time."
"So beautiful it's painful. A very intense and real character study."
"The chronology was off, watching clip of him that you know is from '85 but the story line was at 91."
"This movie just didn't capture that. It was disconnected and hard to really get into."
"I thought the pace of the film was perfect. I don't think it was too slow or that there were any unnecessary scenes."
"This movie was sooooo boring it was painful. I cannot believe all the hype."
"Some bad casting decisions ruined parts of the film."
"I really want a hopeful ending to this and the parents reaction when she is found just isn't what I would expect."
"I only watched this because it was written by the writer of Beasts of the Southern Wild..."
"This is one of those painful movies that stays with you for quite awhile."
"My heart hurt knowing how the film would end and hoping it just wouldn't."
"This was so hard to watch that I had to take a few breaks."

summary: The user shows a strong preference for psychological thrillers and dramas, often valuing emotional depth and resonance in films. They have mixed feelings about narrative complexity and pacing, indicating a nuanced taste that appreciates both engaging storytelling and character studies.
